• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Exploring the New York Housing Market: A Dashboard Case Study

Xiaoying Ma

06/23/2026


1 Introduction

This assignment focuses on building interactive dashboards and story points using Tableau Public. The goal is to help users explore a New York housing data set and better understand patterns in housing prices across different locations, property types, and property characteristics. The data set contains listing-level information such as broker titles, house types, prices, bedrooms, bathrooms, property square footage, addresses, administrative and local areas, street names, and geographic coordinates.

The dashboard is designed to support visual exploration of housing market patterns in New York, with particular attention to location, price variation, property type, and housing size.

Variable Name Description
BROKERTITLE Title of the broker
TYPE Type of the house
PRICE Price of the house
BEDS Number of bedrooms
BATH Number of bathrooms
PROPERTYSQFT Square footage of the property
ADDRESS Full address of the house
STATE State of the house
MAIN_ADDRESS Main address information
ADMINISTRATIVE_AREA_LEVEL_2 Administrative area level 2 information
LOCALITY Locality information
SUBLOCALITY Sublocality information
STREET_NAME Street name
LONG_NAME Long name
FORMATTED_ADDRESS Formatted address
LATITUDE Latitude coordinate of the house
LONGITUDE Longitude coordinate of the house

2 Data Story and Prototyping

2.1 Data Story Formulation

Goal: Help users explore New York housing listings to understand how housing prices vary across neighborhoods, property types, and property size.

Data Story is based on the following 4 questions:

  • Q1: Where are housing listings located across New York, and which areas appear to have relatively higher prices?
  • Q2: Which sublocalities have the highest average housing prices?
  • Q3: How does the distribution of housing prices differ across property types?
  • Q4: What is the relationship between property size and housing price?

2.2 Dashboard Prototyping

Individual Carts Corresponding to Questions

  • Chart Q1 - New York Housing Listings Map – Geographic distribution of listings:
    • Visual: Interactive map showing housing listings across New York using latitude and longitude.
    • Encoding:Each point represents a listing; color indicates relative price level.
    • Interactivity: Hover reveals listing details such as address, price, beds, baths, property size, and sublocality.
    • Why start here: Provides an immediate spatial overview of the housing market and highlights areas with relatively higher and lower prices.
  • Chart Q2 -Top 10 Average Prices by SubLocality – Ranked Bar Chart:
    • Visual: Horizontal bar chart showing the top 10 sublocalities with the highest average housing prices.
    • Interactivity: Hover displays exact average prices; users can visually compare neighborhood-level price differences.
    • Insight example:Some sublocalities, such as Dumbo or Manhattan, may show substantially higher average listing prices than others.
  • Chart Q3 - Property Type Price Distribution – Box Plot:
    • Visual:Box plots of log-transformed housing prices across major property types.
    • Axes: X = property type, Y = log price.
    • Interactivity:Hover can reveal summary information for each category.
    • Why useful: Shows variation in pricing across property types and highlights differences in medians, spread, and outliers.
  • Chart Q4 - Property Size vs. Price – Scatter Plot:
    • Visual: Scatter plot of property square footage against log-transformed housing price, colored by property type.
    • Axes: X = property square footage, Y = log price.
    • Interactivity: Hover displays listing-level details; filtering by type or beds helps users focus on specific market segments.
    • Insight example: Larger properties generally tend to have higher prices, although substantial variation remains across property types.

Interconnection Flow - How charts talk to each other

Action Effect
Select listings on the map Other charts can be interpreted in the context of the selected geographic region
Focus on a high-price sublocality in chart 2 Users can compare that neighborhood with the broader market shown in the map and scatter plot
Examine a property type in chart 3 Users can compare its price distribution with its pattern in the scatter plot
Filter by property type or beds in chart 4 The dashboard narrows attention to specific housing segments

Dashboard Layout

+------------------------------------------------+
|  Title: New York Housing Market Dashboard      |
+------------------------------------------------+
|  [Chart Q1]           |  [Chart Q2]            |
| Housing Listings Map  |  Top 10 Avg. Prices    |
|                       |  by SubLocality        |
+--------------------+---------------------------+
|  [Chart Q3]           |  [Chart Q4]            | 
|Log Price Distribution |  Log Price vs.         | 
|by Property Type       |  Property Size         |                                
+------------------------------------------------+
|  Price legend / notes / optional filters       |
+------------------------------------------------+

3 Tableau Implementation - Step-by-step Procedure

📥 Data Preparation

  • Download/Prepare data (New York housing dataset)

  • Open Tableau Public → Connect → Text/Excel → Select your file

  • Create a new worksheet for each chart in the dashboard

  • Check variable types: PRICE, BEDS, BATH, PROPERTYSQFT, LATITUDE, and LONGITUDE should be numeric.TYPE, LOCALITY, SUBLOCALITY, ADDRESS, and FORMATTED_ADDRESS should be dimensions.

  • Create calculated field with name Log Price:LOG([PRICE])

  • Optional cleaning/filtering for property type: Keep the main property categories, such as: House for sale Condo for sale Co-op for sale Townhouse for sale Multi-family home for sale

  • Set geographic roles: Right-click LATITUDE → Geographic Role → Latitude Right-click LONGITUDE → Geographic Role → Longitude

🗺️ Chart Q1 – New York Housing Listings Map

Step Action where in UI?
1 Create a new worksheet → name it 1_Housing_Map Worksheet tab
2 Drag LONGITUDE to Columns Columns shelf
3 Drag LATITUDE to Rows Rows shelf
4 Change Mark type to Circle Marks card dropdown
5 Drag ADDRESS to Detail Marks card
6 Drag Log Price to Color Marks card
7 Adjust Opacity Color → Opacity
8 Add tooltip fields: PRICE, TYPE, BEDS, BATH,PROPERTYSQFT, SUBLOCALITY Tooltip
9 Edit map title to New York Housing Listings Map Title

Note: Blue may be used to indicate higher prices and orange to indicate lower prices if a diverging palette is selected..


📊 Chart Q2 – Top 10 Average Prices by SubLocality (Bar Chart)

Step Action
1 Create a new worksheet → name it 2_Top10_SubLocality
2 Drag SUBLOCALITY to Rows
3 Drag PRICE to Columns
4 Change aggregation from SUM to AVG
5 Sort bars from largest to smallest
6 Drag AVG(PRICE) to Color
7 Filter SUBLOCALITY to Top 10 by AVG(PRICE)
8 Add labels
9 Format axis as Currency
10 Edit title to Top 10 Average Prices by SubLocality


📦 Chart 3 – Log Price Distribution by Property Type (Box Plot)

Step Action
1 Create a new worksheet → name it 3_Type_BoxPlot
2 Drag TYPE to Columns
3 Drag Log Price to Rows
4 Go to Show Me → choose Box-and-Whisker Plot
5 Go to Analysis and uncheck Aggregate Measures
6 Drag TYPE to Color
7 Add tooltip fields such as AVG(PRICE), AVG(PROPERTYSQFT), AVG(BEDS), and AVG(BATH)
8 Edit title to Log Price Distribution by Property Type

📉 Chart 4 – Log Price vs. Property Size (Scatter Plot)

Step Action
1 Create a new worksheet → name it 4_Size_Price_Scatter
2 Drag PROPERTYSQFT to Columns
3 Drag Log Price to Rows
4 Change Mark type to Circle
5 Drag TYPE to Color
6 Drag ADDRESS or FORMATTED_ADDRESS to Detail
7 Adjust opacity to around 50%–70% for overlapping points
8 Add tooltip fields: PRICE, TYPE, BEDS, BATH, PROPERTYSQFT, SUBLOCALITY
9 edit axis ranges to zoom into the main data region
10 Edit title to Log Price vs. Property Size

4 Dashboard Assembly

Step Action
Create Dashboard
1 Click New Dashboard → name New York Housing Market Dashboard
2 Set dashboard size: Desktop → 1000 x 800 (or Automatic)
Add Sheets to Dashboard
3 Add a title: New York Housing Market Dashboard
4 Drag New York Housing Listings Map → top-left
5 Drag Top 10 Average Prices by SubLocality → top-right
6 Drag Log Price Distribution by Property Type → Bottom-left
7 Drag Log Price vs. Property Size → Bottom-right
8 Publish it to Tableau Public


4.1 Dashboard Interactivity

Action Effect
Hover over points in the map Explore the scatter plot in chart 4
View top-priced sublocalities in chart 2 Compare neighborhood-level price differences
Compare property types in chart 3 Examine differences in price distribution by type
Explore the scatter plot in chart 4 Observe the relationship between size and log-transformed price

4.2 Interactive Tableau Dashboard

View the interactive New York Housing Market Dashboard on Tableau Public


Remarks: Two Important Remarks for Embedding a Published Dashboard into an R Markdown Document

  • Use a single-cell HTML table for embedding: It is recommended to create a single-cell HTML table and place the dashboard embedding code inside that cell. This approach makes it easier to control the alignment and positioning of the dashboard within the report.

  • Clean the copied embedding code before use: To avoid embedding errors, the code copied from the published dashboard should be cleaned and reformatted as shown below. This ensures that the dashboard is displayed correctly in the R Markdown document.


In summary, by integrating both numerical and categorical variables, the dashboard allows users to explore the New York housing market from multiple perspectives. The visualizations include a geographic listing map, a ranked bar chart of average prices by sublocality, a box plot of log-transformed prices by property type, and a scatter plot of property size versus log-transformed price. Through interactive exploration, users can examine how location, neighborhood, property type, and housing size are associated with listing prices.
